sources.document: CNBC news article (9 December 2020) — 'Pfizer jab warning for people with history of significant allergic reactions,' reporting the UK MHRA alert
sources.cited_for: The MHRA's December 9, 2020 alert that anyone advised to carry an adrenaline auto-injector should not receive the Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine

sources.document: Pfizer/BioNTech press release (14 December 2020) on the Pfizer.com newsroom
sources.cited_for: Pfizer/BioNTech's statement that severe allergic reactions had been reported after Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine in mass vaccinations outside of clinical trials, and the contraindication for those with severe allergic reaction history

sources.document: EMA — Comirnaty Risk Management Plan (RMP) summary, version dated 25 December 2020 (archived via web.archive.org)
sources.cited_for: Anaphylaxis classified as an important identified risk in December 2020

sources.document: FDA/CDC presentation by Tom Shimabukuro (CDC ACIP/VSD meeting, June 10, 2021) — vaccine safety update with pre-specified outcome events within 21 days of injection
sources.cited_for: The CDC's June 2021 table limiting adverse-event counting to within 21 days after injection, used to argue real-world studies under-detect long-tail harms

sources.document: McKinsey & Company — 'Ten lessons from the first two years of COVID-19' (McKinsey Healthcare insights, 2022)
sources.cited_for: McKinsey's claim that the vaccine development paradigm has been transformed and that there is serious discussion of cutting sequence-to-authorization time to just 100 days for the next emerging threat

sources.document: FDA — Pfizer C4591001 clinical study report / safety data for 12-15 year olds (BNT162b2 adolescent trial), FDA media download
sources.cited_for: The SAE-section quote describing Maddie de Garay's case categorized as 'functional abdominal pain' and the 1,131-participant adolescent safety evaluation

sources.document: Siri & Glimstad LLP (Aaron Siri) — Letter to Federal Health Agencies Regarding Maddie [de Garay] and Clinical Trials for Children (October 2021)
sources.cited_for: The account of Maddie de Garay's injury after her second dose in the Pfizer pediatric trial

sources.document: Stephanie de Garay (@shdegaray73) — Twitter/X post dated December 3, 2022
sources.cited_for: Stephanie de Garay's confirmation of Maddie's final diagnoses (CIDP, small fiber neuropathy, POTS), supporting the omitted-adverse-event claim

sources.document: Washington Post (Washington Post Live) — transcript: 'WP Subscriber Exclusive: Albert Bourla, author of Moonshot — Inside Pfizer's Nine-Month Race to Make the Impossible Possible,' March 10, 2022
sources.cited_for: Albert Bourla's direct admission that mRNA 'never delivered a single product until that day, not vaccine, not any other medicine' — i.e. mRNA had never been used in a marketed product before.

sources.document: French Wikipedia — biographical article on Albert Bourla
sources.cited_for: Bourla's credentials (veterinary degree and Ph.D. in reproductive biotechnology), supporting context for the statement.

sources.document: Assemblée nationale (OPECST) — interim/draft report on COVID-19 vaccine adverse events ('rapport EI vaccins COVID'), published June 9, 2022
sources.cited_for: The June 9, 2022 OPECST rapporteurs' interim report acknowledging victims' grievances and calling for transparent communication on adverse events and stronger reporting action.

sources.document: Assemblée nationale (OPECST) — information report 'The Covid-19 Vaccine Strategy' / 'La stratégie vaccinale contre la Covid-19,' dated December 15, 2020
sources.cited_for: The earlier OPECST report authored by the same rapporteurs, establishing the parliamentary body Cotton testified to before the June 2022 follow-up.

sources.document: YouTube — video of Brianne Dressen's testimony (her account of injury after AstraZeneca trial dose, including writing goodbye letters)
sources.cited_for: Brianne Dressen's own public testimony describing her severe adverse reaction during the AstraZeneca clinical trial.

sources.document: PubMed (NCBI) — article co-authored by Augusto Roux and Brianne Dressen on vaccine-induced injuries / clinical trial adverse events
sources.cited_for: Published article by Roux and Dressen documenting that physicians confirmed his injuries were vaccine-induced; ties the trial-injury cases together.

sources.document: YouTube — video of Brianne Dressen's hearing/testimony before US Senator Ron Johnson
sources.cited_for: Brianne Dressen being heard by US Senator Ron Johnson — her public testimony in a US Senate setting.

sources.document: FDA — Pfizer/BioNTech BNT162b2 clinical data for adolescents (12-15 yrs) / Maddie de Garay SAE narrative, FDA media document (April 2021)
sources.cited_for: The adolescent-trial safety evaluation included only 1,131 participants — too few to detect myocarditis during the trial

sources.document: FDA — Pfizer/BioNTech clinical report for adolescents aged 12-15 and young people under 25, FDA media document (released April 2021)
sources.cited_for: The adolescent (12-15 yr) clinical report disclosing serious adverse events used as the basis for the myocarditis-undetectability claim

sources.document: FDA — Pfizer/BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine pediatric (6 months-4 years / under-5) clinical data, FDA media document (2022)
sources.cited_for: No myo/pericarditis reported among the more than 3,000 children vaccinated, but insufficient numbers to detect them

sources.document: FDA — Pfizer/BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine 5-11 years FDA briefing/clinical document presented to VRBPAC, FDA media document (26 October 2021)
sources.cited_for: The 5-11 yr reports made public on the FDA website since October 26, 2021

sources.document: FDA — Pfizer/BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine 5-11 years FDA briefing/clinical document presented to VRBPAC, FDA media document (26 October 2021)
sources.cited_for: The 5-11 yr reports made public on the FDA website since October 26, 2021

sources.document: FDA — Vaccines and Related Biological Products Advisory Committee (VRBPAC) October 26, 2021 meeting announcement
sources.cited_for: The VRBPAC meeting at which Pfizer presented the 5-11 yr reports to request marketing approval

sources.document: ClinicalTrials.gov — Pfizer/BioNTech pediatric trial C4591007 (NCT04816643), registry record
sources.cited_for: The pediatric (5-11 yr) clinical trial slated to conclude October 2024 from which the results stem

sources.document: EMA — Comirnaty EPAR Summary of Product Characteristics / Product Information (current, undated version)
sources.cited_for: The Comirnaty SmPC as the essential document detailing fertility, pregnancy and breastfeeding information; basis of the pregnancy clash

sources.document: EMA — Comirnaty EPAR Product Information / SmPC, archived version of 24 December 2020 (via Wayback Machine)
sources.cited_for: The December 2020 Comirnaty SmPC Warnings and Precautions section as posted on the EMA website at authorization

sources.document: EMA — Pfizer Comirnaty Risk Management Plan (RMP) summary, 25 December 2020 (archived via Wayback Machine)
sources.cited_for: As of December 2020 there were no data on efficacy/safety for pregnant or breastfeeding women (use in pregnancy listed as missing information)

sources.document: HAS (Haute Autorité de Santé) — Vaccination strategy: place of the mRNA vaccine Comirnaty (BNT162b2), opinion of December 2020 (modified February 2021)
sources.cited_for: HAS opinion stating that, per the SmPC, BNT162b2 should be used in pregnancy only if benefits outweigh risks; not given during breastfeeding

sources.document: UK Government (MHRA) — Summary Public Assessment Report for Pfizer/BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine, section 3.4 Toxicology
sources.cited_for: UK assessment stating it is not possible to provide sufficient assurances of safety in pregnant women due to absence of reproductive toxicity data

sources.document: Christine Cotton — expert report on the Pfizer/BioNTech clinical trial, completed mid-January 2022 (commissioned by Canadian lawyer Gloriane Blais for the Quebec Court of Appeal)
sources.cited_for: The January 2022 expert report Cotton wrote for lawyer Gloriane Blais, who was subsequently ordered to undergo a psychiatric evaluation

sources.document: FDA — Pfizer/BioNTech BNT162b2 clinical report / VRBPAC efficacy results table (PCR-confirmed symptomatic COVID-19), 10 December 2020 (92-page report)
sources.cited_for: The 8 vs. 162 PCR-confirmed symptomatic case counts (0.0439% vs. 0.8840%) and the reported 95% relative efficacy that Cotton reproduces and recomputes

sources.document: Public Health and Medical Professionals for Transparency (PHMPT) — court-released Pfizer C4591001 documents and SAS/XPT datasets (FOIA litigation led by Aaron Siri)
sources.cited_for: The 'data made publicly available by Mr. Siri' (the court-released SAS database) used by Cotton to recompute efficacy on the anti-nucleocapsid serology criterion (~53%)

sources.document: OpenVAET (Substack) — 'Pfizer/BioNTech C4591001 Trial Audit'
sources.cited_for: Confirmation in the database that vaccinated participants were tested less frequently than the placebo group, biasing the COVID-19 case count and the efficacy recomputation

sources.document: OPECST (Office parlementaire d'évaluation des choix scientifiques et technologiques) — interim report on adverse events of COVID-19 vaccines, published 9 June 2022
sources.cited_for: The OPECST rapporteurs' interim report describing the benefit/risk balance as 'difficult to mathematize' and acknowledging the need for transparent communication on adverse events — the report that omitted Cotton's testimony

sources.document: OPECST / Assemblée nationale — information report 'The Covid-19 Vaccine Strategy' (La stratégie vaccinale contre la Covid-19), 15 December 2020
sources.cited_for: The earlier OPECST report whose authors Cotton addressed at the 5 April 2022 teleconference, quoting its admissions on unknown transmission impact and unestablished efficacy in over-75s

sources.document: PHMPT — court-released Pfizer C4591001 SAS/XPT 'transport' datasets (CDISC-format clinical database), released following the FDA FOIA court decision (Aaron Siri)
sources.cited_for: The SAS database Cotton reprogrammed to count the Argentine site recruitment (more than 5,700 participants, with 10,000 across only 5 centers) and to note the datasets do not follow CDISC standards

sources.document: YouTube — video relating to the Argentine site / Augusto Roux case
sources.cited_for: The recruitment volume and concerns about the Argentinean center where Augusto Roux was enrolled

sources.document: PubMed — peer-reviewed article (PMID 36710689) on the Pfizer C4591001 trial data integrity
sources.cited_for: Supporting the claim of data-quality concerns at the high-volume Argentinean center

sources.document: DavidHealy.org — 'Disappeared in Argentina' (account of Augusto Roux's case at the Cosme Argerich Central Military Hospital)
sources.cited_for: Augusto Roux publicly revealing his medical documents and adverse events from the Argentine military-hospital trial site

sources.document: VaccineHarm (WordPress) — 'Augusto German Roux', 29 December 2020
sources.cited_for: Augusto Roux's testimony and medical documents on his adverse reactions after the Pfizer trial at the Cosme Argerich military hospital

sources.document: OpenVAET (Substack) — "Pfizer/BioNTech C4591001 Trial Audit", independent re-analysis of the court-released C4591001 SAS datasets
sources.cited_for: Confirmation in the database that vaccine-arm participants were tested (PCR) less frequently than placebo-arm participants, biasing the COVID-19 case count in favor of the vaccine

sources.document: Pfizer C4591001 trial data made publicly available via Aaron Siri (the court-released SAS datasets; the data Cotton recalculates from)
sources.cited_for: Cotton's recalculation on the serological (anti-nucleocapsid) criterion yielding ~53% efficacy on ALL COVID cases rather than the announced 95%

sources.document: Public Health and Medical Professionals for Transparency (PHMPT) — phmpt.org, the site posting the FDA's court-ordered released C4591001 clinical-trial documents
sources.cited_for: The FDA being forced by court decision (via Aaron Siri) to release the clinical-trial documents, which are posted publicly; provenance of the data Cotton works from

sources.document: Complaint, PHMPT/de Garay v. FDA, filed 2022-10-11 (hosted on phmpt.org) — the litigation seeking the de Garay family's trial records
sources.cited_for: The Garay family seeking information about their daughter's clinical-trial participation through legal channels; PHMPT v. FDA litigation

sources.document: Siri & Glimstad LLP (Aaron Siri) — "Letter to Federal Health Agencies Regarding Maddie and Clinical Trials for Children", Oct 2021
sources.cited_for: Aaron Siri's letter to the FDA on behalf of the de Garay family describing Maddie de Garay's injury; establishes Siri's role in the litigation

sources.document: EMA - Comirnaty (Pfizer/BioNTech) Risk Management Plan summary (RMP), version dated 25 December 2020 (archived via Wayback Machine, captured 14 Feb 2021)
sources.cited_for: The EMA/Pfizer RMP table listing the 'Missing information' populations and risks (pregnancy/breastfeeding, immunocompromised, frail patients with comorbidities, autoimmune/inflammatory disorders, interaction with other vaccines, long-term safety) on which Cotton's argument that whole populations were vaccinated without data rests (Pages 37-38).

sources.document: EMA - Comirnaty (Pfizer/BioNTech) Risk Management Plan summary (RMP), version dated 25 December 2020 (archived via Wayback Machine, captured 14 Feb 2021)
sources.cited_for: The RMP 'Summary of Safety Issues' table of December 2020 listed risks and missing information (Illustration 16), supporting that there was insufficient safety/efficacy data for pregnant women, immunocompromised, frail comorbid patients, etc. (Page 160).

sources.document: Pfizer - C4591001 Clinical Protocol, November 2020 (BNT162b2 Phase 1/2/3 study protocol)
sources.cited_for: The Pfizer clinical protocol's exclusion of pregnant/breastfeeding women ('Reason for exclusion: To avoid use in a vulnerable population...') quoted on page 58 of Pfizer's documentation, used to show that population was deliberately untested (Page 160).

sources.document: FDA - draft guidance 'Pregnant Women: Scientific and Ethical Considerations for Inclusion in Clinical Trials - Guidance for Industry'
sources.cited_for: FDA guidance noting that drug-use-in-pregnancy information is generally collected post-marketing, supporting Cotton's point that pregnant women were not studied pre-authorization (Page 160).

sources.document: The BMJ (British Medical Journal) - Paul D. Thacker, 'Covid-19: Researcher blew the whistle on data integrity issues in Pfizer's vaccine trial' (BMJ 2021;375:n2635), published 2 November 2021
sources.cited_for: The BMJ investigation in which Brook Jackson detailed Ventavia's data-integrity violations across the Texas trial sites ('Pfizer Gate'), supporting the call for an independent multi-center audit (Page 102).

sources.document: Ventavia Research Group - corporate website
sources.cited_for: Identifying Ventavia Research Group, the contract research organization that employed Brook Jackson and ran the three Texas trial sites (Page 101).

sources.document: Twitter/X account of Dr. Lt. Col. Theresa Long (@ltctheresalong)
sources.cited_for: Reference to fellow whistleblower Theresa Long in the passage introducing Brook Jackson and the Ventavia case (Page 100).

sources.document: iambrookjackson.com - Brook Jackson case documents page (litigation document repository for U.S. ex rel. Jackson v. Ventavia/Pfizer)
sources.cited_for: The public repository of court documents tracking the status of Brook Jackson's qui tam legal battle against Ventavia and Pfizer (ongoing since January 2021) (Page 182).

sources.document: Verity France - website of the French association of COVID-19 vaccine victims (founded by Frederic Beltra and other bereaved families)
sources.cited_for: Identifying the Verity France patient/victim association whose president Cotton contacted for adverse-event figures and who co-signed work submitted to authorities (Pages 33, 40).

sources.document: DavidHealy.org blog — "Disappeared in Argentina" (account of Augusto Roux, the Argentine C4591001 trial participant whose serious adverse event was reclassified in the database)
sources.cited_for: The Argentine-center data-integrity case: Augusto Roux's adverse events being misrecorded as a personal-reason withdrawal, the context for re-examining the released SAS/XPT files.

sources.document: VaccineHarm (WordPress) — "Augusto German Roux" post, 29 Dec 2020 (Roux's public disclosure of his medical documents from the Argentine Pfizer trial site)
sources.cited_for: Augusto Roux publicly revealing his medical documents to expose data manipulation at the Argentine recruiting center.

sources.document: PubMed (NCBI) — peer-reviewed article co-authored by Augusto Roux and Brianne Dressen documenting that four doctors confirmed his injuries were vaccine-induced (PMID 36710689)
sources.cited_for: Independent medical confirmation of Roux's vaccine-induced injuries, contradicting the trial database's reclassification of his withdrawal.
